thingId.description: Identifier of the Thing under test

suiteName.description: Name of the generated test suite

id.description: Unique identifier for this test case

functionName.description: Sanitized function name to use in generated code

interactionName.description: Name of the property or action being tested

httpMethod.description: HTTP method for the generated test request (e.g. GET)

url.description: Resolved endpoint URL for the HTTP request

requestBody.description: JSON-serialized request body for write operations

responseSchema.description: JSON-serialized schema of the expected response payload

propertyPath.description: Dot-path to the sub-property being tested (e.g. 'brightness', 'status.brightness'). Present only for tests derived from object sub-properties.

securityScheme.description: Resolved security scheme type for this test case (e.g. 'nosec'). Determines authentication behavior in generated code.

expectedStatus.description: Expected HTTP response status code for this test case. Defaults to 200 when absent. Set to 4xx for boundary tests that expect the service to reject an out-of-bounds value.

expectedResponseBodyMinimum.description: Minimum bound for the numeric response body. When present, the generated test will parse the response body and assert it is >= this value.

expectedResponseBodyMaximum.description: Maximum bound for the numeric response body. When present, the generated test will parse the response body and assert it is <= this value.

expectedResponseMinLength.description: Minimum length for the response body string. When present, the generated test will assert that the response body length is >= this value.

expectedResponseMaxLength.description: Maximum length for the response body string. When present, the generated test will assert that the response body length is <= this value.

expectedResponseMinItems.description: Minimum number of items for the response body array. When present, the generated test will assert that the response body array length is >= this value.

expectedResponseMaxItems.description: Maximum number of items for the response body array. When present, the generated test will assert that the response body array length is <= this value.
